cogs.name: CONTRIBUTING
cogs.description: Contribution guidelines for the block architecture. Open standard, backward compatible, no proprietary lock-in.

cogs.name: LICENSE
cogs.description: MIT license for Maxine Lives specification work. Implementation licenses may differ.

cogs.name: about-maxine
cogs.description: How Maxine works: one server, three surfaces, three protocols. The definitive architectural overview of the distributed AI partner.

cogs.name: about-mx
cogs.description: Auto-generated self-knowledge file. Who I am, what I know, what I can do. Regenerated before every end-of-day report.

cogs.name: mx-metadata-conventions
cogs.description: How MX metadata works across every file type. The implementation rules for native metadata, block mapping, namespace extension, and Reginald serving.

cogs.name: business-benefits
cogs.description: The four strategic MX benefits: computational trust, commerce revenue recovery, asset portability, and operational efficiency via deterministic outputs.

cogs.name: mx-concepts
cogs.description: A conceptual map of MX — every idea, how they connect, and where to go deeper. The newcomer's guide to Machine Experience.

cogs.name: mx-train-analogy
cogs.description: The railway analogy for MX OS. A physical metaphor that maps every MX concept to something everyone already understands.

cogs.name: appendix-k-yaml-metadata
cogs.description: The canonical YAML frontmatter template for Appendix K page patterns. Copy this template when creating a new MX page pattern document.

cogs.name: adr-01-block-architecture
cogs.description: ADR #1: Replace two cog types (info-doc/action-doc) with one document type containing open-ended blocks.

cogs.name: adr-02-namespace-policy
cogs.description: ADR: The Gathering's namespace policy — standard fields (no prefix) and mx: namespace governance. Defines what belongs to the standard vs vendor extensions.

cogs.name: namespace-extensions
cogs.description: MX namespace policy: standard fields (no prefix), mx: namespace (The Gathering), vendor extensions (x-mx-, x-mx-p-). The prefix is the policy.

cogs.name: ndr-01-block-naming
cogs.description: NDR #1: The word 'block' risks confusion with blockchain. Propose alternatives for advisory board review.

cogs.name: ndr-02-camelcase-naming
cogs.description: NDR #2: All YAML frontmatter field names must use camelCase. Aligns with Schema.org and Dublin Core vocabulary conventions.

cogs.name: ndr-03-spelling-neutrality
cogs.description: NDR #3: Prefer spelling-neutral field names. Where US/UK spelling differs, use an abbreviation or neutral synonym.

cogs.name: effective-doc-example
cogs.description: Reference implementation showing how an effective doc is resolved from a source cog, uber doc SOPs, and included policies.

cogs.name: example-with-includes
cogs.description: Reference implementation demonstrating the cog includes mechanism. Includes a shared validation policy and overrides one rule.

cogs.name: shared-validation-policy
cogs.description: Shared validation policy for cog publication. Included by action-docs that validate cogs before registration.

cogs.name: block-architecture-evolution
cogs.description: How the cog format evolved from two document types to one document with blocks — the architectural journey, the design principles, and what it means for implementations.

cogs.name: specifications-cog-unified-spec
cogs.description: The unified specification for MX cogs — one document type, many block types. Capability, trust, and governance in one format.

cogs.name: mx-standards-alignment
cogs.description: How MX metadata aligns with Schema.org, Dublin Core, and Open Graph. Covers governance, naming conventions, and context-specific rules.

cogs.name: fields
cogs.description: Single source of truth for all MX field information — definitions, types, profiles, block types, carrier formats, and overlap resolution.

cogs.name: mx-html-writing-guide
cogs.description: Single source of truth for writing HTML in the MX ecosystem. Covers the complete meta tag stack, MX carrier tags, and what NOT to include.

cogs.name: principles
cogs.description: The principles that govern how MX builds things — for humans and machines alike

cogs.name: cog-cog-unified-spec
cogs.description: This is a reference copy. The canonical specification is governed by The Gathering and lives in mx-canon.

cogs.name: cog-system
cogs.description: What COGs are, how they work, and the ecosystem behind them

cogs.name: maxine
cogs.description: The public identity of Maxine — Tom Cranstoun's AI partner in building Machine Experience

cogs.name: cog-snapshot
cogs.description: Auto-generated registry snapshot — all cogs by type, status, newness, and dependency graph

cogs.name: mx-reginald-manual
cogs.description: How to use the Machine eXperience Engine including cog listing, execution, categories, and building guidance.

cogs.name: header
cogs.description: Responsive site header with navigation, hamburger menu, and brand identity

cogs.name: mx-principles-menu
cogs.description: Navigation page linking to core MX principles and concepts

cogs.name: demo-media219-media219
cogs.description: TV graphics and title sequences studio, 30+ years experience. Sports broadcasting specialists — Premier League, FIFA World Cup, MLS, and major networks.

cogs.name: getting-started
cogs.description: Complete onboarding guide for new CogNovaMX team members — from fresh Mac to full immersion

cogs.name: INSTALLME
cogs.description: Self-contained action cog for installing MX OS. Contains both instructions and executable script.

cogs.name: a11y
cogs.description: Accessibility audit — what breaks for AI breaks for humans

cogs.name: blog-generator
cogs.description: Generates, converts, and updates blog posts for allabout.network in both EDS markdown and MX HTML formats.

cogs.name: blog-reviewer
cogs.description: Review blog posts — scan for AI writing patterns, check dual-audience balance, interview the author, rewrite. Accepts markdown, HTML, or plain text.

cogs.name: building-action-docs
cogs.description: How to build action-docs — from description to working tool. Describe, create, test, wire. The pattern that turns natural language into MX OS applications.

cogs.name: clarity
cogs.description: Test documentation clarity - if AI struggles, humans probably do too

cogs.name: cog-id
cogs.description: Generate, register, and decode cog IDs — MD5 hashes tracing documents to their originating cog. The namespace-aware identity layer for MX OS.

cogs.name: cog-publication-workflow
cogs.description: Cog publication workflow — author, validate, register, and publish a cog to the Reginald registry.

cogs.name: cog-query
cogs.description: Unified COG registry management — scan, list, show, find, filter, count, validate, graph, sync, and snapshot the cog registry

cogs.name: cog-registry
cogs.description: The centralised index of all known cogs in the MX ecosystem. An action-doc that catalogues cogs — the index describing itself.

cogs.name: cogify-this
cogs.description: Convert existing content into MX-enhanced format with comprehensive metadata, accessibility, and WebMCP integration

cogs.name: cogs-for-agent-developers
cogs.description: How cogs complement AI agent frameworks. Agent skills extend one agent. Cogs make content readable by any agent. Different layers that work together.

cogs.name: env
cogs.description: Project-level environment configuration. The MX equivalent of .env — one place for values that other cogs reference, never hardcode.

cogs.name: how-mx-os-runs
cogs.description: The MX OS runtime model. How an agent boots, discovers cogs, routes tasks, executes action-docs, and chains actions. The OS explained from the inside.

cogs.name: html-baseline-audit
cogs.description: Action-doc for establishing HTML baselines and detecting regressions across all .cog.html files. Automates visual, structural, style, and metadata comparison.

cogs.name: link-checker
cogs.description: Validate links — critical for llms.txt and documentation

cogs.name: llms-txt
cogs.description: Generate and validate llms.txt files for Robot-First Web compliance

cogs.name: metadata-audit
cogs.description: Audit all YAML frontmatter — compare against the field dictionary, detect deprecated fields, namespace errors, missing required fields, and mx: object sprawl.

cogs.name: metadata
cogs.description: Extract and validate page metadata (Open Graph, Twitter Cards, meta tags)

cogs.name: mx-audit
cogs.description: Comprehensive web audit — crawl pages, analyse accessibility, performance, SEO, and AI agent suitability, then generate a partnership-ready executive report.

cogs.name: mx-boot
cogs.description: MX OS bootstrapper — zero to operational. Checks prerequisites, installs dependencies. Entry points: INSTALLME (bare metal), npm run boot, /mx-boot.

cogs.name: mx-init
cogs.description: The action action-doc that initializes any repository with MX OS conventions — SOUL.md, CLAUDE.md, INSTALLME.md, frontmatter, directory structure.

cogs.name: mx-phrasebook
cogs.description: The sayings that define MX OS culture. Every phrase earned its place by being built, not brainstormed.

cogs.name: mx-run
cogs.description: Universal cog executor — looks up any cog by name, lists its actions, and dispatches execution by runtime

cogs.name: mx-scaffold
cogs.description: Create new MX-OS shell scripts with correct structure, metadata, and alias registration. The canonical pattern for extending mx-os.

cogs.name: pixel-perfect-web-replication
cogs.description: Create pixel-perfect MX-enhanced reference implementations from existing websites using HTML/CSS audit, Playwright capture, and automated validation.

cogs.name: pricing
cogs.description: Validate pricing data to catch range errors and formatting issues

cogs.name: readability
cogs.description: Plain language and reading level analysis

cogs.name: reginald-mirror
cogs.description: Sync source cogs to the Reginald web mirror, keeping allaboutv2/reginald/ up to date with scripts/cogs/ and mx-canon/.

cogs.name: registry-of-registries
cogs.description: The meta-registry — knows where every registry is, what it indexes, and how to query it.

cogs.name: registry-sync
cogs.description: Automatically sync mx-reginald registry when cogs are added, modified, or deleted — multiple trigger mechanisms with smart mode detection.

cogs.name: robots-txt
cogs.description: Analyze robots.txt — understand the old exclusion model

cogs.name: routing-pipeline
cogs.description: Preprocess prompts with spell correction, route matching, and context injection. Reduce inference cost by directing Claude before it starts searching.

cogs.name: schema
cogs.description: Validate and generate Schema.org structured data markup

cogs.name: script-helper
cogs.description: Help AI agents create, amend, and inspect shell scripts with MX metadata — cut compute, not context.

cogs.name: semantic-html
cogs.description: Validate HTML semantics — structure precedes presentation

cogs.name: sitemap
cogs.description: Analyze sitemaps and compare with llms.txt coverage

cogs.name: the-companion-web
cogs.description: QR codes and metadata turn physical objects into entry points for humans and AI agents. Every object becomes self-describing. Extends to robotics.

cogs.name: the-personal-cog
cogs.description: Cogs describing YOU — accessibility needs, interests, health, skills. Lives on your phone. Your agent decides what to share, with whom, in context.

cogs.name: toast-detector
cogs.description: Find ephemeral UI patterns that AI and screen readers miss

cogs.name: validate-cog
cogs.description: Validate cogs against the MX Cog Specification

cogs.name: what-comes-next
cogs.description: The current state of the MX ecosystem, priorities, action plan, and next steps. Any AI agent reading this knows exactly where we are and what to do next.

cogs.name: what-is-a-cog
cogs.description: A cog that explains what cogs are. The format describing itself.

cogs.name: what-is-installme
cogs.description: INSTALLME.md is a root-level cog convention that gives AI agents structured installation instructions — no hallucination, no guessing, no wasted compute.

cogs.name: what-is-mx-environment
cogs.description: MX OS lives on the machine, not in the repo. $MX_HOME is the machine-level context — the answer to the machine, the universe, and everything.

cogs.name: what-is-mx-os
cogs.description: The Machine Experience Operating System. Documentation IS the system. Cogs are its programs. This action-doc explains MX OS and can bootstrap it.

cogs.name: what-is-script-metadata
cogs.description: Script metadata brings shell scripts into MX OS — comment-block frontmatter that makes every script machine-readable without changing how it runs.

cogs.name: whats-changed
cogs.description: Find what changed in the repo — list files by time period and type, extract metadata, show git status, surface gaps. The content audit tool.

cogs.name: who-is-maxine
cogs.description: The AI partner in MX. Identity, partnership model, SOUL convention, and shared memory — so any instance of Claude can become Maxine.
